object.value: personal data

condition.value: processing is necessary for the performance of a task carried out in the public interest or in the exercise of official authority vested in the controller

classification_rationale: Lawfulness ground, same shape as the adopted H03 record for sibling point (a).
anchor: (e) processing is necessary for the performance of a task carried out in the public interest
paragraph_text: (e) processing is necessary for the performance of a task carried out in the public interest or in the exercise of official authority vested in the controller;
parent_texts: 1. Processing shall be lawful only if and to the extent that at least one of the following applies:

object.value: personal data referred to in paragraph 1

condition.value: for the purposes referred to in point (h) of paragraph 2 when those data are processed by or under the responsibility of a professional subject to the obligation of professional secrecy under Union or Member State law or rules established by national competent bodies or by another person also subject to an obligation of secrecy under Union or Member State law or rules established by national competent bodies

classification_rationale: Professional-secrecy condition on the health-care processing ground — MigrainePredict-core (clinician processing).
anchor: Personal data referred to in paragraph 1 may be processed for the purposes referred to in point (h) of paragraph 2
paragraph_text: 3. Personal data referred to in paragraph 1 may be processed for the purposes referred to in point (h) of paragraph 2 when those data are processed by or under the responsibility of a professional subject to the obligation of professional secrecy under Union or Member State law or rules established by national competent bodies or by another person also subject to an obligation of secrecy under Union or Member State law or rules established by national competent bodies.

object.value: the information to be provided to data subjects pursuant to Articles 13 and 14 in combination with standardised icons

condition.value: in order to give in an easily visible, intelligible and clearly legible manner a meaningful overview of the intended processing

classification_rationale: Icons permission; information duties' bearer is the controller.
anchor: The information to be provided to data subjects pursuant to Articles 13 and 14 may be provided in combination with standardised icons
paragraph_text: 7. The information to be provided to data subjects pursuant to Articles 13 and 14 may be provided in combination with standardised icons in order to give in an easily visible, intelligible and clearly legible manner a meaningful overview of the intended processing. Where the icons are presented electronically they shall be machine-readable.

object.value: that the icons are machine-readable

condition.value: where the icons are presented electronically

classification_rationale: Machine-readability requirement on electronically presented icons.
anchor: Where the icons are presented electronically they shall be machine-readable.
paragraph_text: 7. The information to be provided to data subjects pursuant to Articles 13 and 14 may be provided in combination with standardised icons in order to give in an easily visible, intelligible and clearly legible manner a meaningful overview of the intended processing. Where the icons are presented electronically they shall be machine-readable.

object.value: a copy of the personal data undergoing processing

classification_rationale: Copy duty under the access right; right-holder in beneficiary per the adopted convention.
anchor: The controller shall provide a copy of the personal data undergoing processing.
paragraph_text: 3. The controller shall provide a copy of the personal data undergoing processing. For any further copies requested by the data subject, the controller may charge a reasonable fee based on administrative costs. Where the data subject makes the request by electronic means, and unless otherwise requested by the data subject, the information shall be provided in a commonly used electronic form.

object.value: a reasonable fee based on administrative costs

condition.value: for any further copies requested by the data subject

classification_rationale: Fee permission for further copies.
anchor: For any further copies requested by the data subject, the controller may charge a reasonable fee
paragraph_text: 3. The controller shall provide a copy of the personal data undergoing processing. For any further copies requested by the data subject, the controller may charge a reasonable fee based on administrative costs. Where the data subject makes the request by electronic means, and unless otherwise requested by the data subject, the information shall be provided in a commonly used electronic form.

object.value: the information in a commonly used electronic form

condition.value: where the data subject makes the request by electronic means, and unless otherwise requested by the data subject

classification_rationale: Format duty for electronic requests.
anchor: the information shall be provided in a commonly used electronic form
paragraph_text: 3. The controller shall provide a copy of the personal data undergoing processing. For any further copies requested by the data subject, the controller may charge a reasonable fee based on administrative costs. Where the data subject makes the request by electronic means, and unless otherwise requested by the data subject, the information shall be provided in a commonly used electronic form.

object.value: the lifting of the restriction of processing

condition.value: before the restriction of processing is lifted

beneficiary.value: a data subject who has obtained restriction of processing pursuant to paragraph 1

classification_rationale: Advance-notice duty; the informed party goes in beneficiary (T29/H09 convention), the implicit content (the lift) in object as CONTEXT.
anchor: A data subject who has obtained restriction of processing pursuant to paragraph 1 shall be informed by the controller before the restriction of processing is lifted.
paragraph_text: 3. A data subject who has obtained restriction of processing pursuant to paragraph 1 shall be informed by the controller before the restriction of processing is lifted.

object.value: other tasks and duties

classification_rationale: Plain permission.
anchor: The data protection officer may fulfil other tasks and duties.
paragraph_text: 6. The data protection officer may fulfil other tasks and duties. The controller or processor shall ensure that any such tasks and duties do not result in a conflict of interests.

object.value: that any such tasks and duties do not result in a conflict of interests

classification_rationale: Conflict-of-interest safeguard on the same permission.
anchor: The controller or processor shall ensure that any such tasks and duties do not result in a conflict of interests.
paragraph_text: 6. The data protection officer may fulfil other tasks and duties. The controller or processor shall ensure that any such tasks and duties do not result in a conflict of interests.

object.value: the contact point for the supervisory authority on issues relating to processing, including the prior consultation referred to in Article 36, and to consult, where appropriate, with regard to any other matter

classification_rationale: DPO task-list item; the parent chapeau ('shall have at least the following tasks') makes each item a duty.
anchor: (e) to act as the contact point for the supervisory authority on issues relating to processing
paragraph_text: (e) to act as the contact point for the supervisory authority on issues relating to processing, including the prior consultation referred to in Article 36, and to consult, where appropriate, with regard to any other matter.
parent_texts: 1. The data protection officer shall have at least the following tasks:

object.value: a member of the supervisory authority

condition.value: only in cases of serious misconduct or if the member no longer fulfils the conditions required for the performance of the duties

classification_rationale: 'Shall be dismissed only in cases X' = dismissal permitted only on those grounds; the dismissing organ is supplied by Member State law — subject attribution reviewable.
anchor: A member shall be dismissed only in cases of serious misconduct
paragraph_text: 4. A member shall be dismissed only in cases of serious misconduct or if the member no longer fulfils the conditions required for the performance of the duties.

object.value: the awareness of controllers and processors of their obligations under this Regulation

condition.value: on its territory

classification_rationale: Supervisory-authority task-list item.
anchor: (d) promote the awareness of controllers and processors of their obligations under this Regulation;
paragraph_text: (d) promote the awareness of controllers and processors of their obligations under this Regulation;
parent_texts: 1. Without prejudice to other tasks set out under this Regulation, each supervisory authority shall on its territory:

object.value: that its supervisory authority shall have the power to bring infringements of this Regulation to the attention of the judicial authorities and where appropriate, to commence or engage otherwise in legal proceedings

condition.value: in order to enforce the provisions of this Regulation

classification_rationale: Member-State duty to equip the supervisory authority.
anchor: Each Member State shall provide by law that its supervisory authority shall have the power
paragraph_text: 5. Each Member State shall provide by law that its supervisory authority shall have the power to bring infringements of this Regulation to the attention of the judicial authorities and where appropriate, to commence or engage otherwise in legal proceedings, in order to enforce the provisions of this Regulation.

object.value: reimbursement from another Member State in relation to damage referred to in paragraph 4

condition.value: in the case provided for in paragraph 1, without prejudice to the exercise of its rights vis-à-vis third parties and with the exception of paragraph 5

classification_rationale: 'Shall refrain from' = prohibition; carve-outs stay in the condition.
anchor: each Member State shall refrain ... from requesting reimbursement from another Member State
paragraph_text: 6. Without prejudice to the exercise of its rights vis-à-vis third parties and with the exception of paragraph 5, each Member State shall refrain, in the case provided for in paragraph 1, from requesting reimbursement from another Member State in relation to damage referred to in paragraph 4.

object.value: court proceedings for exercising the right to receive compensation before the courts competent under the law of the Member State referred to in Article 79(2)

classification_rationale: Venue rule for compensation claims; passive, the claimant (data subject) is the implied actor — subject attribution reviewable.
anchor: Court proceedings for exercising the right to receive compensation shall be brought before the courts competent under the law of the Member State referred to in Article 79(2).
paragraph_text: 6. Court proceedings for exercising the right to receive compensation shall be brought before the courts competent under the law of the Member State referred to in Article 79(2).

object.value: administrative fines in addition to, or instead of, measures referred to in points (a) to (h) and (j) of Article 58(2)

condition.value: depending on the circumstances of each individual case

classification_rationale: Fine-imposition frame; the imposing actor is the supervisory authority (Article 58(2)(i) power).
anchor: Administrative fines shall, depending on the circumstances of each individual case, be imposed in addition to, or instead of, measures
paragraph_text: 2. Administrative fines shall, depending on the circumstances of each individual case, be imposed in addition to, or instead of, measures referred to in points (a) to (h) and (j) of Article 58(2). When deciding whether to impose an administrative fine and deciding on the amount of the administrative fine in each individual case due regard shall be given to the following:

object.value: the criteria set out in the points of this paragraph

condition.value: when deciding whether to impose an administrative fine and deciding on the amount of the administrative fine in each individual case

classification_rationale: Chapeau of the criteria list; the operative criteria live in points (a)-(k) (T52-style frame, not NA).
anchor: due regard shall be given to the following:
paragraph_text: 2. Administrative fines shall, depending on the circumstances of each individual case, be imposed in addition to, or instead of, measures referred to in points (a) to (h) and (j) of Article 58(2). When deciding whether to impose an administrative fine and deciding on the amount of the administrative fine in each individual case due regard shall be given to the following:

object.value: compliance with measures referred to in Article 58(2) previously ordered against the controller or processor concerned with regard to the same subject-matter

condition.value: when deciding whether to impose an administrative fine and deciding on the amount of the administrative fine

classification_rationale: Fine-criteria list item (content-list convention).
anchor: (i) where measures referred to in Article 58(2) have previously been ordered against the controller or processor concerned
paragraph_text: (i) where measures referred to in Article 58(2) have previously been ordered against the controller or processor concerned with regard to the same subject-matter, compliance with those measures;
parent_texts: 2. Administrative fines shall, depending on the circumstances of each individual case, be imposed in addition to, or instead of, measures referred to in points (a) to (h) and (j) of Article 58(2). When deciding whether to impose an administrative fine and deciding on the amount of the administrative fine in each individual case due regard shall be given to the following:

definition.value: a set of technical specifications as defined in Article 2, point (4) of Regulation (EU) No 1025/2012, providing means to comply with certain requirements established under this Regulation

classification_rationale: Definitions-article entry, value verbatim without the ''X' means' prefix.
anchor: (28) 'common specification' means a set of technical specifications
paragraph_text: (28) ‘common specification’ means a set of technical specifications as defined in Article 2, point (4) of Regulation (EU) No 1025/2012, providing means to comply with certain requirements established under this Regulation;
parent_texts: For the purposes of this Regulation, the following definitions apply:

definition.value: the automated recognition of physical, physiological, behavioural, or psychological human features for the purpose of establishing the identity of a natural person by comparing biometric data of that individual to biometric data of individuals stored in a database

classification_rationale: Definitions-article entry; biometric-data adjacent, MigrainePredict profile dimension.
anchor: (35) 'biometric identification' means the automated recognition of physical, physiological, behavioural, or psychological human features
paragraph_text: (35) ‘biometric identification’ means the automated recognition of physical, physiological, behavioural, or psychological human features for the purpose of establishing the identity of a natural person by comparing biometric data of that individual to biometric data of individuals stored in a database;
parent_texts: For the purposes of this Regulation, the following definitions apply:

subject.value: deployers of high-risk AI systems that are public authorities, or Union institutions, bodies, offices or agencies

object.value: the registration obligations referred to in Article 49

classification_rationale: Registration duty for public-authority deployers. applies_to_healthcare=False on the public-authority framing; a public hospital deploying MigrainePredict would flip this — reviewable.
anchor: Deployers of high-risk AI systems that are public authorities ... shall comply with the registration obligations referred to in Article 49.
paragraph_text: 8. Deployers of high-risk AI systems that are public authorities, or Union institutions, bodies, offices or agencies shall comply with the registration obligations referred to in Article 49. When such deployers find that the high-risk AI system that they envisage using has not been registered in the EU database referred to in Article 71, they shall not use that system and shall inform the provider or the distributor.

subject.value: deployers of high-risk AI systems that are public authorities, or Union institutions, bodies, offices or agencies

object.value: that system

condition.value: when such deployers find that the high-risk AI system that they envisage using has not been registered in the EU database referred to in Article 71

classification_rationale: Use ban on unregistered systems; same deployers carried from the first sentence.
anchor: they shall not use that system
paragraph_text: 8. Deployers of high-risk AI systems that are public authorities, or Union institutions, bodies, offices or agencies shall comply with the registration obligations referred to in Article 49. When such deployers find that the high-risk AI system that they envisage using has not been registered in the EU database referred to in Article 71, they shall not use that system and shall inform the provider or the distributor.

subject.value: deployers of high-risk AI systems that are public authorities, or Union institutions, bodies, offices or agencies

object.value: the provider or the distributor

condition.value: when such deployers find that the high-risk AI system that they envisage using has not been registered in the EU database referred to in Article 71

classification_rationale: Companion duty in the same sentence as the use ban.
anchor: and shall inform the provider or the distributor
paragraph_text: 8. Deployers of high-risk AI systems that are public authorities, or Union institutions, bodies, offices or agencies shall comply with the registration obligations referred to in Article 49. When such deployers find that the high-risk AI system that they envisage using has not been registered in the EU database referred to in Article 71, they shall not use that system and shall inform the provider or the distributor.

object.value: the market surveillance authority of the results of the assessment, submitting the filled-out template referred to in paragraph 5 of this Article as part of the notification

condition.value: once the assessment referred to in paragraph 1 of this Article has been performed

classification_rationale: Fundamental-rights-impact-assessment notification duty. hc=False: the Article 27 assessment trigger is deployer-type specific, outside MigrainePredict's operative basis — reviewable.
anchor: the deployer shall notify the market surveillance authority of its results
paragraph_text: 3. Once the assessment referred to in paragraph 1 of this Article has been performed, the deployer shall notify the market surveillance authority of its results, submitting the filled-out template referred to in paragraph 5 of this Article as part of the notification. In the case referred to in Article 46(1), deployers may be exempt from that obligation to notify.

predicate.value: notify the market surveillance authority of the results of the assessment

object.value: that obligation to notify

condition.value: in the case referred to in Article 46(1)

classification_rationale: Express exemption from the notification duty for the Article 46(1) emergency-authorisation case.
anchor: In the case referred to in Article 46(1), deployers may be exempt from that obligation to notify.
paragraph_text: 3. Once the assessment referred to in paragraph 1 of this Article has been performed, the deployer shall notify the market surveillance authority of its results, submitting the filled-out template referred to in paragraph 5 of this Article as part of the notification. In the case referred to in Article 46(1), deployers may be exempt from that obligation to notify.

object.value: that AI systems intended to interact directly with natural persons are designed and developed in such a way that the natural persons concerned are informed that they are interacting with an AI system

condition.value: unless this is obvious from the point of view of a natural person who is reasonably well-informed, observant and circumspect, taking into account the circumstances and the context of use

classification_rationale: Transparency duty; the obviousness carve-out stays in the condition of the primary norm (no mirror statement, per the adopted exception convention).
anchor: Providers shall ensure that AI systems intended to interact directly with natural persons are designed and developed in such a way that the natural persons concerned are informed
paragraph_text: 1. Providers shall ensure that AI systems intended to interact directly with natural persons are designed and developed in such a way that the natural persons concerned are informed that they are interacting with an AI system, unless this is obvious from the point of view of a natural person who is reasonably well-informed, observant and circumspect, taking into account the circumstances and the context of use. This obligation shall not apply to AI systems authorised by law to detect, prevent, investigate or prosecute criminal offences, subject to appropriate safeguards for the rights and freedoms of third parties, unless those systems are available for the public to report a criminal offence.

predicate.value: ensure that natural persons are informed that they are interacting with an AI system

object.value: this obligation

condition.value: AI systems authorised by law to detect, prevent, investigate or prosecute criminal offences, subject to appropriate safeguards for the rights and freedoms of third parties, unless those systems are available for the public to report a criminal offence

classification_rationale: Separate-sentence disapplication for law-enforcement systems, with its own public-reporting counter-exception kept inside the condition.
anchor: This obligation shall not apply to AI systems authorised by law to detect, prevent, investigate or prosecute criminal offences
paragraph_text: 1. Providers shall ensure that AI systems intended to interact directly with natural persons are designed and developed in such a way that the natural persons concerned are informed that they are interacting with an AI system, unless this is obvious from the point of view of a natural person who is reasonably well-informed, observant and circumspect, taking into account the circumstances and the context of use. This obligation shall not apply to AI systems authorised by law to detect, prevent, investigate or prosecute criminal offences, subject to appropriate safeguards for the rights and freedoms of third parties, unless those systems are available for the public to report a criminal offence.

object.value: the Commission without delay and in any event within two weeks after that requirement is met or it becomes known that it will be met

condition.value: where a general-purpose AI model meets the condition referred to in Article 51(1), point (a)

classification_rationale: Systemic-risk threshold notification (general-purpose AI chapter, outside MigrainePredict's scope).
anchor: the relevant provider shall notify the Commission without delay and in any event within two weeks
paragraph_text: 1. Where a general-purpose AI model meets the condition referred to in Article 51(1), point (a), the relevant provider shall notify the Commission without delay and in any event within two weeks after that requirement is met or it becomes known that it will be met. That notification shall include the information necessary to demonstrate that the relevant requirement has been met. If the Commission becomes aware of a general-purpose AI model presenting systemic risks of which it has not been notified, it may decide to designate it as a model with systemic risk.

object.value: the information necessary to demonstrate that the relevant requirement has been met

condition.value: in that notification

classification_rationale: Content requirement on the same notification.
anchor: That notification shall include the information necessary to demonstrate that the relevant requirement has been met.
paragraph_text: 1. Where a general-purpose AI model meets the condition referred to in Article 51(1), point (a), the relevant provider shall notify the Commission without delay and in any event within two weeks after that requirement is met or it becomes known that it will be met. That notification shall include the information necessary to demonstrate that the relevant requirement has been met. If the Commission becomes aware of a general-purpose AI model presenting systemic risks of which it has not been notified, it may decide to designate it as a model with systemic risk.

object.value: the general-purpose AI model as a model with systemic risk

condition.value: if the Commission becomes aware of a general-purpose AI model presenting systemic risks of which it has not been notified

classification_rationale: Commission's own-motion designation power.
anchor: it may decide to designate it as a model with systemic risk
paragraph_text: 1. Where a general-purpose AI model meets the condition referred to in Article 51(1), point (a), the relevant provider shall notify the Commission without delay and in any event within two weeks after that requirement is met or it becomes known that it will be met. That notification shall include the information necessary to demonstrate that the relevant requirement has been met. If the Commission becomes aware of a general-purpose AI model presenting systemic risks of which it has not been notified, it may decide to designate it as a model with systemic risk.

subject.value: the AI Office and the Board

object.value: that the codes of practice cover the identification of the type and nature of the systemic risks at Union level, including their sources, where appropriate

classification_rationale: Codes-of-practice content item; the parent chapeau places the aim-to-ensure duty on the AI Office and the Board.
anchor: (c) the identification of the type and nature of the systemic risks at Union level
paragraph_text: (c) the identification of the type and nature of the systemic risks at Union level, including their sources, where appropriate;
parent_texts: 2. The AI Office and the Board shall aim to ensure that the codes of practice cover at least the obligations provided for in Articles 53 and 55, including the following issues:

object.value: the originally collected data

condition.value: only in accordance with Union data protection law

classification_rationale: Sandbox personal-data condition arm: restricted sharing permission.
anchor: providers can further share the originally collected data only in accordance with Union data protection law
paragraph_text: (e) providers can further share the originally collected data only in accordance with Union data protection law; any personal data created in the sandbox cannot be shared outside the sandbox;
parent_texts: 1. In the AI regulatory sandbox, personal data lawfully collected for other purposes may be processed solely for the purpose of developing, training and testing certain AI systems in the sandbox when all of the following conditions are met:

object.value: any personal data created in the sandbox

condition.value: outside the sandbox

classification_rationale: Flat ban in the same point; duty-bearer carried from the first clause.
anchor: any personal data created in the sandbox cannot be shared outside the sandbox
paragraph_text: (e) providers can further share the originally collected data only in accordance with Union data protection law; any personal data created in the sandbox cannot be shared outside the sandbox;
parent_texts: 1. In the AI regulatory sandbox, personal data lawfully collected for other purposes may be processed solely for the purpose of developing, training and testing certain AI systems in the sandbox when all of the following conditions are met:

applies_to.value: Union or national law on the testing in real world conditions of high-risk AI systems related to products covered by Union harmonisation legislation listed in Annex I

classification_rationale: Savings clause (T07 shape): sectoral testing law — including the MDR, MigrainePredict's route — remains applicable alongside this paragraph.
anchor: This paragraph shall be without prejudice to Union or national law on the testing in real world conditions
paragraph_text: This paragraph shall be without prejudice to Union or national law on the testing in real world conditions of high-risk AI systems related to products covered by Union harmonisation legislation listed in Annex I.
parent_texts: 1. Testing of high-risk AI systems in real world conditions outside AI regulatory sandboxes may be conducted by providers or prospective providers of high-risk AI systems listed in Annex III, in accordance with this Article and the real-world testing plan referred to in this Article, without prejudice to the prohibitions under Article 5.

object.value: a post-market monitoring system in a manner that is proportionate to the nature of the AI technologies and the risks of the high-risk AI system

classification_rationale: Core post-market monitoring duty; directly MigrainePredict-relevant.
anchor: Providers shall establish and document a post-market monitoring system
paragraph_text: 1. Providers shall establish and document a post-market monitoring system in a manner that is proportionate to the nature of the AI technologies and the risks of the high-risk AI system.

object.value: the competent authorities, and where relevant with the notified body concerned

condition.value: during the investigations referred to in the first subparagraph

classification_rationale: Serious-incident investigation cooperation duty.
anchor: The provider shall cooperate with the competent authorities
paragraph_text: The provider shall cooperate with the competent authorities, and where relevant with the notified body concerned, during the investigations referred to in the first subparagraph, and shall not perform any investigation which involves altering the AI system concerned in a way which may affect any subsequent evaluation of the causes of the incident, prior to informing the competent authorities of such action.
parent_texts: 6. Following the reporting of a serious incident pursuant to paragraph 1, the provider shall, without delay, perform the necessary investigations in relation to the serious incident and the AI system concerned. This shall include a risk assessment of the incident, and corrective action.

object.value: any investigation which involves altering the AI system concerned in a way which may affect any subsequent evaluation of the causes of the incident

condition.value: prior to informing the competent authorities of such action

classification_rationale: Evidence-preservation ban in the same sentence.
anchor: shall not perform any investigation which involves altering the AI system concerned
paragraph_text: The provider shall cooperate with the competent authorities, and where relevant with the notified body concerned, during the investigations referred to in the first subparagraph, and shall not perform any investigation which involves altering the AI system concerned in a way which may affect any subsequent evaluation of the causes of the incident, prior to informing the competent authorities of such action.
parent_texts: 6. Following the reporting of a serious incident pursuant to paragraph 1, the provider shall, without delay, perform the necessary investigations in relation to the serious incident and the AI system concerned. This shall include a risk assessment of the incident, and corrective action.

subject.value: the law enforcement, immigration or asylum authorities

object.value: the technical documentation referred to in Annex IV within the premises of those authorities

condition.value: when the law enforcement, immigration or asylum authorities are providers of high-risk AI systems referred to in point 1, 6 or 7 of Annex III

classification_rationale: Passive 'shall remain' duty; the bearer is the authority-provider named in the same sentence.
anchor: the technical documentation referred to in Annex IV shall remain within the premises of those authorities
paragraph_text: When the law enforcement, immigration or asylum authorities are providers of high-risk AI systems referred to in point 1, 6 or 7 of Annex III, the technical documentation referred to in Annex IV shall remain within the premises of those authorities. Those authorities shall ensure that the market surveillance authorities referred to in Article 74(8) and (9), as applicable, can, upon request, immediately access the documentation or obtain a copy thereof. Only staff of the market surveillance authority holding the appropriate level of security clearance shall be allowed to access that documentation or any copy thereof.
parent_texts: 3. Without prejudice to paragraphs 1 and 2, information exchanged on a confidential basis between the national competent authorities or between national competent authorities and the Commission shall not be disclosed without prior consultation of the originating national competent authority and the deployer when high-risk AI systems referred to in point 1, 6 or 7 of Annex III are used by law enforcement, border control, immigration or asylum authorities and when such disclosure would jeopardise public and national security interests. This exchange of information shall not cover sensitive operational data in relation to the activities of law enforcement, border control, immigration or asylum authorities.

subject.value: the law enforcement, immigration or asylum authorities

object.value: that the market surveillance authorities referred to in Article 74(8) and (9), as applicable, can, upon request, immediately access the documentation or obtain a copy thereof

classification_rationale: Access guarantee toward market surveillance.
anchor: Those authorities shall ensure that the market surveillance authorities ... can, upon request, immediately access the documentation
paragraph_text: When the law enforcement, immigration or asylum authorities are providers of high-risk AI systems referred to in point 1, 6 or 7 of Annex III, the technical documentation referred to in Annex IV shall remain within the premises of those authorities. Those authorities shall ensure that the market surveillance authorities referred to in Article 74(8) and (9), as applicable, can, upon request, immediately access the documentation or obtain a copy thereof. Only staff of the market surveillance authority holding the appropriate level of security clearance shall be allowed to access that documentation or any copy thereof.
parent_texts: 3. This exchange of information shall not cover sensitive operational data in relation to the activities of law enforcement, border control, immigration or asylum authorities.

object.value: that documentation or any copy thereof

condition.value: only staff holding the appropriate level of security clearance shall be allowed access

anchor: Only staff of the market surveillance authority holding the appropriate level of security clearance shall be allowed to access
paragraph_text: When the law enforcement, immigration or asylum authorities are providers of high-risk AI systems referred to in point 1, 6 or 7 of Annex III, the technical documentation referred to in Annex IV shall remain within the premises of those authorities. Those authorities shall ensure that the market surveillance authorities referred to in Article 74(8) and (9), as applicable, can, upon request, immediately access the documentation or obtain a copy thereof. Only staff of the market surveillance authority holding the appropriate level of security clearance shall be allowed to access that documentation or any copy thereof.

Without prejudice to paragraphs 1 and 2, information exchanged on a confidential basis between the national competent authorities or between national competent authorities and the Commission shall not be disclosed without prior consultation of the originating national competent authority and the deployer when high-risk AI systems referred to in point 1, 6 or 7 of Annex III are used by law enforcement, border control, immigration or asylum authorities and when such disclosure would jeopardise public and national security interests. predicate.value: provide a clear and meaningful explanation of the role of the AI system in the decision-making procedure

object.value: paragraph 1

condition.value: the use of AI systems for which exceptions from, or restrictions to, the obligation under that paragraph follow from Union or national law in compliance with Union law

classification_rationale: Disapplication of the right-to-explanation duty where sectoral law provides exceptions; H08/H19 dispensation shape, relieved duty cited from paragraph 1.
anchor: Paragraph 1 shall not apply to the use of AI systems for which exceptions ... follow from Union or national law
paragraph_text: 2. Paragraph 1 shall not apply to the use of AI systems for which exceptions from, or restrictions to, the obligation under that paragraph follow from Union or national law in compliance with Union law.

object.value: the rules on administrative fines in such a manner that the fines are imposed by competent national courts or by other bodies, as applicable in those Member States

condition.value: depending on the legal system of the Member States

classification_rationale: Institutional flexibility for fine imposition.
anchor: the rules on administrative fines may be applied in such a manner that the fines are imposed by competent national courts
paragraph_text: 9. Depending on the legal system of the Member States, the rules on administrative fines may be applied in such a manner that the fines are imposed by competent national courts or by other bodies, as applicable in those Member States. The application of such rules in those Member States shall have an equivalent effect.

object.value: that the application of such rules has an equivalent effect

classification_rationale: Equivalence requirement on the same flexibility.
anchor: The application of such rules in those Member States shall have an equivalent effect.
paragraph_text: 9. Depending on the legal system of the Member States, the rules on administrative fines may be applied in such a manner that the fines are imposed by competent national courts or by other bodies, as applicable in those Member States. The application of such rules in those Member States shall have an equivalent effect.

applies_to.value: AI systems intended to be used by a judicial authority or on their behalf to assist a judicial authority in researching and interpreting facts and the law and in applying the law to a concrete set of facts, or to be used in a similar way in alternative dispute resolution

classification_rationale: Annex III high-risk listing item (administration of justice area): names a system class in scope of the Article 6(2) high-risk classification.
anchor: (a) AI systems intended to be used by a judicial authority or on their behalf
paragraph_text: (a) AI systems intended to be used by a judicial authority or on their behalf to assist a judicial authority in researching and interpreting facts and the law and in applying the law to a concrete set of facts, or to be used in a similar way in alternative dispute resolution;
parent_texts: High-risk AI systems pursuant to Article 6(2) are the AI systems listed in any of the following areas:
8. Administration of justice and democratic processes:

object.value: that the established quality management system is in compliance with the requirements of Article 17

classification_rationale: Internal-control conformity-assessment step; the annex's indicative 'verifies' states a procedural duty.
anchor: The provider verifies that the established quality management system is in compliance with the requirements of Article 17.
paragraph_text: 2. The provider verifies that the established quality management system is in compliance with the requirements of Article 17.
